sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: The Beneficiary has received multiple nationally recognized awards in the field of communication and market research. Details can be found in Exhibit X.

sections.heading: 1. The Beneficiary's Receipt of the Data Legacy of the Year Award [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
In 2019, the Beneficiary was awarded the "Data Legacy of the Year Award" at the inaugural DATA AWARDS ceremony, as confirmed by the engraved trophy identifying "Liu, Dehuan Peking University" as the recipient. [Exhibit X - x] The DATA AWARDS is described as "one of the most influential awards in the field of mobile Internet," jointly launched by Umeng+ (UBDC), Zhongguancun Big Data Industry Alliance, Tsingdata Big Data Industries Association, and China Big Data Industry Observation. [Exhibit X - x]

Umeng+ is "the Domestic Leading Third-party Intelligent Provider of Multidomain Data," having "provided professional data services over ten years for accumulative 2.3 million mobile applications and 9.5 million websites," with typical customers including CCTV and the People's Daily client. [Exhibit X - x]

Through a rigorous selection process involving a jury of 21 judges from institutions including Tsinghua University and Peking University, who reviewed candidates over a two-month period, only 28 individuals and 40 enterprises were selected across seven award categories. [Exhibit X - x] The Data Legacy of the Year Award specifically honors individuals who "have been working deeply in the field of big data and mobile Internet for many years, spreading their insights and experiences and educating the whole industry," and who "always guide the development of the industry" -- a description that speaks directly to the Beneficiary's sustained contributions to data science. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: 2. The Beneficiary's Receipt of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary's book "Age Theory: Social Time in Social Space" won the third prize of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions (Humanities and Social Sciences), issued by the Ministry of Education of the People's Republic of China on September 4, 2009. [Exhibit X - x] The Ministry of Education is a cabinet-level department of the Chinese government responsible for overseeing all higher education institutions nationwide; accordingly, this award represents the highest level of government-sponsored academic recognition available to researchers in the humanities and social sciences. [Exhibit X - x]

The selection process was rigorous and multi-tiered: candidates were first "evaluat[ed] by experts," then subjected to "public announcement," and finally reviewed by an Award Committee composed of directors and deputy directors of the Social Sciences Committee of the Ministry of Education along with national scholars. [Exhibit X - x] Out of the entire national pool of submissions, "a total of 648 achievements have been awarded, including 38 first prizes, 205 second prizes, 392 third prizes and 13 achievement popularization prizes," demonstrating that the award is both highly competitive and nationally significant. [Exhibit X - x]

Notably, the Beneficiary's distinguished work falls within the Journalism and Communication category, which awarded only 10 third prizes nationwide, placing the Beneficiary among a highly select group of scholars in his discipline. [Exhibit X - x] Fellow awardees in the Journalism and Communication category include scholars from Fudan University, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, and the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, confirming that the Beneficiary's recognition places him among the top communication researchers in China. [Exhibit X - x] The Beneficiary's faculty profile at Peking University further records that "His book Age Theory: Social Time in Social Space won the third prize of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions (Humanities and Social Sciences)," confirming this national honor. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: 3. The Beneficiary's Receipt of the Excellent Paper Award at the 11th China Communication Conference [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary and co-author Chen Cui received the Excellent Paper Award at the 11th China Communication Conference for their paper titled "Challenges Posed by the Internet to Traditional Media in the Time Dimension - A Study on the Possibility of Internet Media Usage Time Surpassing Traditional Media Usage Time," as certified by the Communication Association of China on July 10, 2010. [Exhibit X - x] The 11th China Communication Conference was co-organized by the Communication Association of China, the School of Journalism and Communication of Peking University, and the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, and drew "more than 260 communication scholars from China and abroad." [Exhibit X - x]

The conference attracted scholars and research students from the United States, the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Canada, Switzerland, Sweden, Japan, South Korea, India, the United Arab Emirates, and Hong Kong and Taiwan, demonstrating a genuinely international scope. [Exhibit X - x] The conference organizing committee "organized expert scholars to anonymously evaluate and select 23 excellent academic papers from the more than 200 papers submitted by scholars and students from home and abroad," yielding an acceptance rate of approximately 11.5 percent and confirming the selectivity of this recognition. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: In conclusion, the three awards detailed above are among the most widely recognized and influential national awards in the field of communication and market research, each selected through a credible and rigorous process. By winning these highly competitive awards, the Beneficiary has demonstrated himself to be among the best professionals in the field of communication and market research. Therefore, not only does the Beneficiary meet the plain language of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(i), but he is also one of the small percentage who have risen to the very top of the field.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Dr. Liu has received multiple nationally recognized awards in the sociology field profession. Details can be found in Exhibit X.

sections.heading: 1. Dr. Liu's Receipt of the Data Legacy of the Year Award by Data Awards [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
Dr. Liu was awarded one of the highest national honors for sociology professionals, the Data Legacy of the Year Award, at the Data Awards in 2019, for his outstanding achievements within the big data industry, which is an important sociological consideration. [Exhibit X - x] The Data Awards, one of the most prestigious awards in the field of big data, was jointly launched by companies including Umeng+ and Zhongguancun Big Data Industry Alliance. [Exhibit X - x]

Umeng+ is a leading domestic third-party intelligence provider of multi-domain data. With its superior technology and advanced computing algorithms, Umeng+ provides internet businesses with comprehensive statistical analysis, operational support, and effective technology improvement tools. Additionally, the company provides brand owners with a suite of online and offline digital intelligent marketing services. To date, Umeng+ holds over sixty intellectual property rights, showcasing its commitment to innovation and industry leadership. [Exhibit X - x] Another sponsor of the Data Awards, the Zhongguancun Big Data Industry Alliance (hereinafter referred to as the "Alliance") is an international and non-profit organization in China, formed voluntarily by big data-related enterprises, institutions, and social organizations. Established in January 2016, it was the first officially registered social organization in China's big data industry. The Alliance actively engages in the promotion and exchange of services in areas such as technological innovation, industrial and financial development, and intellectual property protection. Through these efforts, it has significantly contributed to the construction of China's big data industry ecosystem and the continuous innovation and development of the digital economy. [Exhibit X - x]

The purpose of the awards is to recognize outstanding enterprises and professionals in data construction, outstanding contributions, and the influence of experts, thereby promoting the advancement of data and intelligence within the big data industry. In 2019, the Data Awards assembled a jury of 21 judges, which was comprised of authorities and industry experts from prestigious institutions such as the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology, Tsinghua University, and Peking University. Over a two-month review period, the judges employed professional selection models to evaluate the candidates, ultimately identifying seven categories of Data Awards. As a result, 40 outstanding data enterprises and 28 exceptional data professionals were recognized for their achievements. [Exhibit X - x]

Dr. Liu is one of the distinguished winners under the category of "Data Legacy of the Year Award." To be eligible for consideration in this prestigious category, nominees must not only have worked for many years and been at the forefront of the field of big data and mobile internet, but also have shared their valuable insights and experiences with their industry peers to promote and develop data applications, industry methodologies and scientific research. [Exhibit X - x]

In its 2019 list of winners, Data Awards also recognized the achievements of other outstanding big data industry practitioners and enterprises, such as Dr. Qiji Ma and TikTok. Dr. Qiji Ma, a highly regarded figure in the realm of market research, holds the distinguished position of Researcher at the China National Institute of Advertising. [Exhibit X - x] As one of the earliest practitioners of market research in mainland China and among the first registered consultants in the nation, Dr. Ma also received the "Data Legacy of the Year Award." [Exhibit X - x] On the other hand, TikTok, known worldwide as the leading platform for short-form mobile video, was awarded "Best Data-Driven Growth Award," on the grounds that its continuous data-driven and self-imposed practices have ultimately helped the business achieve significant profit growth. [Exhibit X - x]

sections.heading: 2. Dr. Liu's Receipt of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
Another national award won by Dr. Liu that is widely recognized in the sociology industry, is the third place prize of the Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research of Higher Education Institutions (Humanities and Social Sciences). [Exhibit X - x] All information related to this award is reported on by the official website of the Ministry of Education of the People's Republic of China. The Ministry of Education of the People's Republic of China is a key department entrusted with overseeing and managing a range of educational matters nationwide, which includes basic, vocational, and higher education, among other areas. The main responsibilities of the Ministry of Education include formulating strategies, policies and plans aimed at promoting educational reform and development, as well as ensuring the effective coordination and management of educational efforts at different levels. [Exhibit X - x]

The Award for Outstanding Achievements in Scientific Research in Higher Education Institutions is a prestigious accolade established by the Ministry of Education of the People's Republic of China, aimed at honoring exceptional contributions and accomplishments of scholars within the sphere of humanities and social sciences across higher education institutions nationwide. This significant initiative not only fosters a culture of exploration and innovation by connecting theory with practice, but also catalyzes advancements in disciplinary frameworks, academic viewpoints, and research methodologies. By championing the growth and development of humanities and social sciences in higher education, the award plays a vital role in driving social progress and construction, further cementing its importance in the academic community. [Exhibit X - x]

When the Ministry of Education sent notice regarding the issuance of the award, the strict selection criteria were stated in the appendix. [Exhibit X - x] According to the appendix, the awarded research must exhibit distinct perspectives, comprehensive arguments, insightful information, accurate data, stringent logic, scientific methodologies, and innovation at the forefront of the field. To maintain impartiality, the assessment is conducted through a centralized, independent evaluation process in which expert jury members thoroughly examine the application materials based on established criteria. They provide qualitative and quantitative evaluations, as well as recommendations for award candidates and respective award levels. The Award Committee Office then reviews these recommendations to ensure the utmost integrity and accuracy in the selection process. [Exhibit X - x]

Notably, Dr. Liu's distinguished work, Age Theory: Social Time in Social Space, falls within the Journalism and Communication category, an area that received only 15 out of the 648 total awards presented. [Exhibit X - x] This underscores the rarity and significant recognition associated with Professor Liu's achievement in this particular field, further highlighting the exceptional impact of their research and contributions to the discipline.

sections.heading: 3. Dr. Liu's receipt of the Excellent Paper Award at the 11th China Communication Conference [Exhibit X - x]
sections.content:
Dr. Liu also won the Excellent Paper Award at the 11th China Communication Conference for his paper titled, "Challenges Posed by the Internet to Traditional Media in the Time Dimension -- A Study on the Possibility of Internet Media Usage Time Surpassing Traditional Media Usage Time." [Exhibit X - x] The conference was co-organized by the Communication Association of China, the School of Journalism and Communication of Peking University and the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. [Exhibit X - x]

As reported by MediaResearch.cn, the 11th China Communication Conference, a respected authority in the communication sector, boasted robust international appeal and exemplifies scholarly excellence. This edition of the conference has drawn over 200 renowned scholars from around the globe, including the United States, the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Canada, Switzerland, Sweden, Japan, South Korea, India, and the United Arab Emirates. With its size and scale surpassing all previous editions, this conference stands as the most expansive one yet. At the invitation of the conference, experts in the field of communication including Prof. S. Shyam Sundar and Prof. Milton J. Bennett also attended the conference. [Exhibit X - x] S. Shyam Sundar is the esteemed founder of the Media Effects Research Laboratory, which is recognized as a leading facility of its kind in the United States. In the early stages of Internet-based media research, Prof. Sundar emerged as a pioneer, becoming one of the first to publish refereed research on the subject in the Journal of Communication, the flagship publication of the International Communication Association (ICA). His extensive contributions to the field have led to him to being recognized as the most published author of Internet-related research in the field of communications. [Exhibit X - x] Dr. Milton Bennett, who holds a Ph.D. in Intercultural Communication and Sociology from the University of Minnesota, is a renowned figure in the field of intercultural studies. He co-founded the Intercultural Communication Institute and currently serves as the director of the Intercultural Development Research Institute. [Exhibit X - x]

Throughout the duration of the conference, scholars contributed an impressive total of over 260 academic papers. In a bid to celebrate these exceptional scholarly endeavors, the conference committee orchestrated an anonymous peer review process conducted by expert scholars. Of these submissions, 23 remarkable academic papers were acknowledged at the award ceremony that took place during the conference's grand finale. This recognition undeniably bolsters Dr. Liu's standing as a proponent of academic excellence. [Exhibit X - x]

: The MediaResearch.cn is an academic website hosted by the Institute of Journalism and Communication of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. The purpose of this website is to exchange communication information, observe communication media, analyze communication phenomena, and discuss communication academics.

In conclusion, the three awards mentioned above are among the most widely recognized and influential national awards in the sociology industry. They have adopted a credible selection process to choose the best possible candidates. The honorees are some of the most distinguished leaders in the field. By winning these highly influential awards, Dr. Liu has demonstrated himself to be the best or among the best professionals in the field of sociology. Therefore, not only does Dr. Liu meet the plain language of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(i), but he is also one of the small percentage who have risen to the very top of the sociology industry.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(i), the record establishes that the Beneficiary has received a nationally recognized prize for excellence in his field and that the award was conferred through a selective, expert-driven process designed to honor leaders in data and communications research.

sections.heading: Award Identification and Granting Organizations
sections.content:
The Beneficiary received the 2019 "Data Awards - Data Legacy of the Year Award," and the award certificate identifies him by name, "Liu, Dehuan," together with his affiliation at Peking University [Exhibit X - x]. The granting body's published materials explain that the "Data Awards" was jointly launched by Umeng+, Zhongguancun Big Data Industry Alliance, Tsingdata Big Data Industries Association, and China Big Data Industry Observation [Exhibit X - x]. Those same materials state that the award was created to encourage benchmark enterprises and experts with outstanding contributions and influence in the data industry, and describe the program as "one of the most influential awards in the field of mobile Internet" [Exhibit X - x].

Umeng+ itself is not a minor or ad hoc entity, because its corporate materials describe it as a leading domestic third-party intelligent provider of multidomain data services that, by June 2021, had served more than 2.3 million mobile applications and 9.5 million websites across 191 industry categories [Exhibit X - x]. Umeng+ further states that it had provided professional data services for over ten years and counted major clients such as CCTV, People's Daily's client platform, Toutiao, Himalaya, and Chang Ba, which confirms that the award was administered within a substantial national data-technology ecosystem rather than a small private circle [Exhibit X - x]. In addition, the organization's own materials reflect multiple industry recognitions and security certifications, reinforcing that the awarding framework came from an established and credible actor in China's data-intelligence sector [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Selection Process and Award Criteria
sections.content:
The selection process for the 2019 award was also demonstrably rigorous [Exhibit X - x]. The published announcement states that the first "Data Awards" invited a jury of 21 judges drawn from authorities, industry experts, and scholars, including participants from the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology, Tsinghua University, and Peking University [Exhibit X - x]. The same source explains that the review lasted two months, that a professional selection model was developed, and that seven award categories ultimately emerged from that process [Exhibit X - x]. The record therefore shows a multi-member, expert-based evaluation structure rather than a simple nomination or popularity contest [Exhibit X - x].

The award criteria for the Beneficiary's category were likewise substantive: the "Data Legacy of the Year Award" was reserved for individuals who had worked deeply in big data and mobile Internet for many years, had disseminated their insights and experience, had educated the broader industry, and had guided the industry's development [Exhibit X - x]. That description is significant because it confirms that the award recognized sustained field-level influence and long-term intellectual leadership, not merely routine job performance [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Quantitative Evidence of Selectivity
sections.content:
The quantitative evidence in the exhibits confirms selectivity as well [Exhibit X - x]. The award announcement states that, after the review period, there were 40 winning enterprises and 28 winning individuals across all seven categories [Exhibit X - x]. Although the record does not disclose the total number of nominees or applicants, it does establish that only 28 individuals across all individual award categories were selected after a two-month review by a 21-member jury [Exhibit X - x]. Even without a complete applicant denominator, that evidence still demonstrates rarity because the award was limited to a narrow cohort chosen from a nationally organized program expressly designed to identify influential leaders in the data-intelligence field [Exhibit X - x].

The record further states that, in one cycle, 648 achievements were selected for awards, including 38 first prizes, 205 second prizes, 392 third prizes, and 13 achievement popularization prizes [Exhibit X - x]. However, because the OCR-accessible pages in the present record do not permit reliable extraction of every individualized fact necessary to tie that separate Ministry award to the Beneficiary with the same level of precision as the 2019 Data Legacy award, this section does not rely on that award for its core showing [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Accordingly, the record demonstrates that the Beneficiary has received a nationally recognized prize or award for excellence in the field of endeavor within the meaning of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(i).
